2605.06330.title: Fine-Tuning Small Language Models for Solution-Oriented Windows Event Log Analysis
2605.06330.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) have shown promise for event log analysis, but their high computational requirements, reliance on cloud infrastructure, and security concerns limit practical deployment. In addition, most existing approaches focus only on the identification of the problem and do not provide actionable remediation. Small language models (SLMs) present a light-weight alternative that can be fine-tuned for a specific purpose and hosted locally. This paper investigates whether SLMs, when fine-tuned for a specific task, can serve as a practical alternative for event log analysis while also generating solutions. We first create a large-scale synthetic Windows event log dataset that contains remediation actions using a high-performing LLM. We then fine-tune multiple SLMs and LLMs using the LoRA parameter-efficient fine-tuning technique and evaluate their performance by comparing with expert assessment. The results show that the dataset accurately reflects real-world scenarios and that fine-tuned SLMs consistently outperform LLMs in identifying issues and providing relevant remediation, while requiring fewer computational resources.
2605.06330.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在事件日誌分析中顯示出潛力，但其高計算需求、對雲基礎設施的依賴以及安全問題限制了實際部署。此外，大多數現有方法僅專注於問題的識別，而未提供可行的修復方案。小型語言模型（SLMs）提供了一種輕量級的替代方案，可以針對特定目的進行微調並在本地托管。本文探討了當 SLMs 針對特定任務進行微調時，是否可以作為事件日誌分析的實用替代方案，同時生成解決方案。我們首先使用高效能的 LLM 創建一個包含修復行動的大規模合成 Windows 事件日誌數據集。然後，我們使用 LoRA 參數高效微調技術對多個 SLMs 和 LLMs 進行微調，並通過與專家評估進行比較來評估它們的性能。結果顯示，該數據集準確反映了現實世界的場景，並且經過微調的 SLMs 在識別問題和提供相關修復方面始終優於 LLMs，同時需要更少的計算資源。
2605.06330.author: Siraaj Akhtar et.al.
2605.06330.authors: Siraaj Akhtar, Saad Khan, Simon Parkinson
